subject: Your Free 12-Week Workout Program is Here!
preview_text: 3 phases, 12 weeks, designed for men 35+ -- let's get started
body_html:
FIT OVER 35

Strength. Health. Longevity.

Welcome -- Your 12-Week Program is Ready

Hey there,

Thanks for joining the Fit Over 35 community. You just made a decision that your future self will thank you for.

Your free 12-Week Workout Program is ready. This is the same structured approach that has helped hundreds of men over 35 build real, sustainable strength -- without destroying their joints or spending hours in the gym.

ACCESS YOUR PROGRAM

What's Inside:

Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4) -- Build your base with compound movements, proper form, and progressive overload.

Phase 2: Strength Building (Weeks 5-8) -- Increase intensity with structured strength work, 3-4 days per week.

Phase 3: Performance (Weeks 9-12) -- Push your limits with advanced programming for real transformation.

Why This Works for Men 35+:

  • Joint-friendly progression -- smart warm-ups that protect your body
  • Time-efficient -- 45-60 minutes, 3-4 days per week
  • Recovery-focused -- built-in deload weeks
  • Evidence-based -- grounded in exercise science, not trends
START WEEK 1 TODAY

One piece of advice: trust the process. The first two weeks might feel easy. That is by design.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team

You are receiving this because you signed up at fitover35.com

subject: The #1 Form Mistake Killing Your Gains After 35
preview_text: 3 quick fixes you can use in your next workout
body_text:
Hey {first_name},

Quick question -- did you get a chance to look at your 12-week program yet?

Whether you did or not, I want to talk about something that can make or break your results: exercise form.

Here is the hard truth. After 35, your body does not forgive sloppy reps the way it did at 22. One bad deadlift can sideline you for weeks. I learned this the hard way with a herniated disc at 37 -- all because I was ego-lifting with garbage form.

3 Form Fixes That Prevent Injury and Build More Muscle:

1. Control the eccentric (lowering) phase.
Most guys drop the weight and yank it back up. Instead, take 2-3 seconds on the way down. This is where most of your muscle growth happens, and it protects your tendons and joints.

2. Brace your core on every rep -- not just ab exercises.
Before every squat, every overhead press, every row -- take a breath into your belly and brace like someone is about to punch you in the gut. This stabilizes your spine and prevents the low back injuries that plague guys our age.

3. Ditch the full range of motion ego.
"Full range of motion" does not mean the same thing at 38 as it did at 25. If your hips round at the bottom of a squat, you are going too deep for YOUR mobility. Work within the range you can control with perfect form.

I wrote a full guide on this with video demos for every major lift in the program.

READ THE FULL GUIDE WITH VIDEO DEMOS: https://fitover35.com/blog/exercise-form-guide-over-35

Recovery Tool I Use Daily:
Foam rolling before and after every session keeps my hips, IT band, and shoulders mobile enough to train pain-free.

LuxFit High-Density Foam Roller -- $14.95 on Amazon: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0040EGNIU?tag=fitover3509-20

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team
cta_text: READ THE FULL GUIDE WITH VIDEO DEMOS

subject: What I Eat in a Day to Build Muscle at 38
preview_text: Sample meal plan, macro targets, and the protein timing myth
body_text:
Hey {first_name},

You can follow the best training program in the world, but if your nutrition is off, you will spin your wheels. Here is exactly what a real day of eating looks like for building muscle after 35.

Your Macro Targets (180 lb Male):
- Protein: 160-180g/day (0.8-1g per lb of body weight)
- Carbs: 200-250g/day (fuel your training)
- Fat: 60-80g/day (hormone production -- critical after 35)
- Calories: ~2,400-2,600/day for lean muscle gain

My Actual Day of Eating:

7:00 AM -- Breakfast: 4 whole eggs scrambled with spinach and peppers, 2 slices whole grain toast with avocado, black coffee (~45g protein, 35g carbs, 30g fat)

10:00 AM -- Snack: Greek yogurt with berries and almonds (~25g protein, 20g carbs, 12g fat)

12:30 PM -- Lunch: 8oz chicken breast, large sweet potato, mixed green salad with olive oil (~50g protein, 45g carbs, 15g fat)

3:30 PM -- Pre-Workout: Protein shake with banana and oats (~30g protein, 50g carbs, 5g fat)

5:00 PM -- Training

6:30 PM -- Post-Workout: Protein shake with creatine (~25g protein, 5g carbs, 2g fat)

7:30 PM -- Dinner: 8oz salmon, brown rice, steamed broccoli (~40g protein, 45g carbs, 18g fat)

Daily total: ~215g protein, ~200g carbs, ~82g fat, ~2,400 calories

The Protein Timing Truth:
The "anabolic window" is more like an "anabolic barn door" -- you have 2-3 hours. Hit your daily protein target and spread it across 4-5 meals. That is it.

I put together a free meal prep template that makes planning this out dead simple.

DOWNLOAD THE FREE MEAL PREP TEMPLATE: https://fitover35.com/meal-prep-template

What I Actually Use:
- Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard Whey -- $30: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B000QSNYGI?tag=fitover3509-20 (24g protein per scoop, mixes clean)
- Prep Naturals Glass Meal Prep Containers (5-Pack) -- $25: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0CG7TGQHD?tag=fitover3509-20 (glass, not plastic -- no chemicals leaching)

Eat to fuel the work.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team

subject: The Recovery Secret Nobody Talks About
preview_text: Why what you do OUTSIDE the gym matters more after 35
body_text:
Hey {first_name},

Let me tell you a story. Two years ago, I was training hard -- 5 days a week, heavy compound lifts, feeling great. Then one morning I woke up and could not turn my neck. My shoulder felt like cement. My lower back was on fire.

I had to take 6 weeks off. Six weeks of lost progress because I ignored recovery.

Here is the thing nobody tells you: after 35, you do not grow in the gym. You grow during recovery.

My 10-Minute Recovery Routine (Do This Tonight):

Minutes 1-3: Foam Roll -- Hit your quads, IT band, upper back, and lats. 30 seconds on each side. Slow, deliberate pressure.

Minutes 3-6: Static Stretching -- Hip flexor stretch (90 seconds each side). Pec doorway stretch (60 seconds each side). Tight hip flexors destroy your squat and deadlift form.

Minutes 6-8: Lacrosse Ball Work -- Press a lacrosse ball into your upper traps and the bottom of each foot. These are tension hotspots most people ignore.

Minutes 8-10: Diaphragmatic Breathing -- Lie on your back, knees up, hands on belly. 4-count inhale, 7-count hold, 8-count exhale. This shifts you from fight-or-flight to recovery mode. This is the secret weapon.

TRY THIS 10-MINUTE RECOVERY ROUTINE TONIGHT: https://fitover35.com/blog/10-minute-recovery-routine

Sleep: The Most Anabolic Thing You Can Do
Your testosterone and growth hormone peak during deep sleep. If you are getting less than 7 hours, you are leaving gains on the table. Three things that transformed my sleep:
- Room temperature at 65-68 degrees F
- No screens 45 minutes before bed
- Magnesium glycinate 30 minutes before sleep

My Recovery Arsenal:
- LuxFit High-Density Foam Roller -- $14.95: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0040EGNIU?tag=fitover3509-20
- Bob and Brad Massage Gun -- $49.99: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09JWFQ6GC?tag=fitover3509-20 (deep tissue percussion, 5 speeds, quieter than most)
- Magnesium Glycinate 400mg -- $15.99: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09MFN4C3J?tag=fitover3509-20 (glycinate form is key for sleep and muscle relaxation)

Recovery is not weakness. Recovery is strategy.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team

subject: Your Mindset is Holding You Back (Here's Proof)
preview_text: Why consistency beats intensity every single time
body_text:
Hey {first_name},

About a week into your program now. Let me guess what might be happening in your head:

- "The weights feel too light. Should I go heavier?"
- "I missed a session. Might as well start over."
- "That guy my age on Instagram looks way better."
- "Is this actually going to work?"

Sound familiar? Good. It means you are human. Now let me give you some data.

The Numbers Don't Lie:
- 92% of gym-goers who train 3x/week for 12 weeks see measurable strength gains -- regardless of starting point
- 73% of people who quit a program do so in the first 2 weeks
- Training 3 days at 70% effort beats training 1 day at 100% effort -- every time
- The difference between people who transform and people who don't is not genetics. It is showing up on the days you do not feel like it.

The Habit Stacking Method:

Instead of relying on motivation (which is unreliable), stack your training onto existing habits:

- After I pour my morning coffee -> I put on my gym shoes
- After I park at the gym -> I do 5 minutes of mobility
- After my last set -> I log my workout
- After I shower post-gym -> I prep tomorrow's gym bag

The chain reaction takes the decision-making out of it.

The Identity Shift:
Stop saying "I'm trying to get fit." Start saying "I'm someone who trains." Research shows identity-based habits stick 3x longer than goal-based ones.

We are building a free accountability group for Fit Over 35 members. No Instagram posing. Just guys over 35 showing up, sharing what is working, and keeping each other honest.

JOIN THE FREE ACCOUNTABILITY GROUP: https://fitover35.com/community

You are past the quitting window. Keep going.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team

subject: Week 2 Check-In: How's the Program Going?
preview_text: Quick troubleshooting + answers to your top questions
body_text:
Hey {first_name},

We are about two weeks in. This is where most programs succeed or fail. Here are the most common questions I get at Week 2:

"Phase 1 feels too easy. Can I skip ahead?"
No. Phase 1 builds your movement patterns, connective tissue, and work capacity. Guys who skip ahead are the ones emailing me at Week 6 about shoulder pain. Trust the progression.

"I missed two days this week. Should I restart?"
Absolutely not. Pick up where you left off. A program with missed days still beats no program.

"I am sore ALL the time. Is that normal?"
Some soreness is normal in Weeks 1-2. But if a joint hurts (sharp pain, not muscle ache), stop that exercise and substitute. There is a modifications list on the program page.

"Can I add cardio?"
Yes -- 2-3 sessions of 20-30 minutes (walking, cycling, swimming). Do not run a 10K on your rest days. Cardio should support recovery, not compete with it.

"When will I see results?"
Weeks 1-2: Better energy and sleep. Weeks 3-4: Clothes fit differently. Weeks 6-8: Other people start noticing. Weeks 10-12: You will not recognize your old self.

Quick Modification Guide:
- Bad knees? Swap barbell squats for goblet squats or leg press
- Bad shoulders? Swap overhead press for landmine press
- Bad back? Swap conventional deadlifts for trap bar or Romanian deadlifts
- Bad wrists? Use EZ curl bar instead of straight bar

I have one ask for you today. Hit reply and tell me your biggest win so far. It does not have to be dramatic. Maybe you made it to the gym 3 times. Maybe you ate a high-protein meal. Maybe you just showed up on a day you did not feel like it.

I read every reply. Seriously.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team

Know Someone Who'd Benefit?
If you have a friend, brother, coworker, or training partner over 35 who wants to get stronger -- forward this email to them. The 12-week program is free, and having a partner makes consistency 10x easier.

From here on out, you will hear from me weekly. No spam, no fluff -- just actionable advice that moves the needle.

Thank you for trusting us with your fitness journey.

Stay strong,
The Fit Over 35 Team
